description_long: This house features a steeply pitched gable roof in a muted green color, with a prominent central dormer and exposed wooden beams accenting the upper facade. The exterior walls are finished in a light beige tone, complemented by darker brown structural elements and trim. The front of the house includes a covered porch supported by slender posts, with a set of wide windows beneath the overhang and a single main door. On one side, there is an attached open terrace or deck area, bordered by a low railing, providing additional outdoor space. The windows are arranged in a mostly symmetrical pattern, with warm yellow lighting suggesting interior illumination. The overall design evokes a traditional European or Tudor-inspired architectural style, blending rustic charm with functional urban living.
description: A two-story house with a steep green gable roof, beige walls, exposed wooden beams, and a covered front porch. Features include a side terrace, symmetrical windows, and a warm, inviting appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, covered porch, symmetrical windows, and part of the side terrace.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the open terrace area, steps leading up, and the side profile of the gable roof.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back wall, the full extent of the terrace, and the roof's broad overhang.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a clear look at the secondary entrance, upper window, and the side of the porch.
attribute_entryway_design: Single wooden door with a small overhang, accessed via a short step, and a covered porch supported by slender posts.
